title: Helper Functions
description: Learn how to create reusable code blocks called functions! Write once, use many times.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Welcome to functions! These are like mini-programs inside your program!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: So you can write code ONCE and use it MANY times! Watch this!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Let's create a 'greet' function! Type: def greet():

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: 'def' means 'define' - we're defining a new function!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now add what the function does - with an indent!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Your function is defined! But it won't run until you CALL it!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: To run a function, just type its name with parentheses!

objectives.description: Type: greet() (no indent - outside the function)

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Click play to see your function run!

objectives.description: Click play to run your code

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: It said 'Hello, farmer!'! The function worked!

phases.title: Call It Again!
phases.description: Functions can be called multiple times

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: The power of functions: you can call them many times! Add more calls!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Click play to see the function run 3 times!

objectives.description: Click play to run your code

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: Three greetings! I wrote the print once but it ran 3 times!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: That's the power of functions! Write once, use forever!
